questions.question: Even after Ajanta, very few sites with paintings have survived. What other art forms were also traditionally plastered and painted?
questions.explanation: It may also be noted that the sculptures too were plastered and painted.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which dynasty ruled the Badami region from 543 to 598 CE?
questions.explanation: Badami was the capital of the early Chalukyan dynasty which ruled the region from 543 to 598 CE.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: The Chalukyas established their power in the Deccan following the decline of which rule?
questions.explanation: With the decline of the Vakataka rule, the Chalukyas established their power in the Deccan.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which Chalukya king patronised the excavation of the Badami caves?
questions.explanation: The Chalukya king, Mangalesha, patronised the excavation of the Badami caves.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: To which deity is Cave No.4 at Badami dedicated, giving it its popular name?
questions.explanation: The patron records his Vaishnava affiliation. Therefore, the cave is popularly known as the Vishnu Cave.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where exactly has a fragment of the painting survived in the Vishnu Cave at Badami?
options: On the vaulted roof of the front mandapa
On the floor of the cave
questions.explanation: Only a fragment of the painting has survived on the vaulted roof of the front mandapa.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Who is the figure shown seated inside the palace watching a dance scene in the Badami cave paintings?
questions.explanation: One shows Kirtivarman, the son of Pulakesi I and the elder brother of Mangalesha, seated inside the palace with his wife and feudatories watching a dance scene.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which figures are depicted towards the corner of the palace panel in the Badami cave?
questions.explanation: Towards the corner of the panel are figures of Indra and his retinue.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Stylistically speaking, the Badami cave paintings represent an extension of the mural tradition from which earlier site?
questions.explanation: Stylistically speaking, the painting represents an extension of the tradition of mural painting from Ajanta to Badami in South India.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: How did the artists at Badami create protruding structures and volume on the faces?
options: By carving deep into the rock
By using multiple thick layers of paint
questions.explanation: It is noteworthy to observe that the contours of different parts of the face create protruding structures of the face itself. Thus, with simple line treatment artists could create volume.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which kings succeeded the Chalukya kings in parts of South India and continued to patronise the arts?
questions.explanation: The Pallava kings who succeeded the Chalukya kings in parts of South India, were also patrons of arts.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which Pallava king was responsible for building temples at Panamalai, Mandagapattu, and Kanchipuram?
questions.explanation: Mahendravarma I who ruled in the seventh century was responsible for building temples at Panamalai, Mandagapattu and Kanchipuram.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What does the title 'Vichitrachitta', given to Mahendravarman I, mean?
questions.explanation: The inscription at Mandagapattu mentions Mahendravarman I with numerous titles such as Vichitrachitta (curious-minded).
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which title given to Mahendravarman I translates to 'tiger among artists'?
questions.explanation: Chitrakarapuli (tiger among artists).
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which Pallava king patronised the paintings at the Kanchipuram temple?
questions.explanation: Paintings at the Kanchipuram temple were patronised by the Pallava king, Rajsimha.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: The remaining traces of paintings at the Kanchipuram temple primarily depict which deity?
questions.explanation: Only traces of paintings remain now which depict Somaskanda.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Jaina cave patronised by the Pandya kings?
questions.explanation: Tirumalaipuram caves and Jaina caves at Sittanvasal are some of the surviving examples.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: Where are the dancing figures of celestial nymphs prominently seen in the Sittanvasal caves?
options: On the pillars of the veranda
On the floor of the shrine
questions.explanation: On the pillars of the veranda are seen dancing figures of celestial nymphs.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: What color is used for the firmly drawn contours of the figures at Sittanvasal?
questions.explanation: The contours of figures are firmly drawn and painted in vermilion red on a lighter background.
questions.subject: Fine Arts

questions.question: In what color is the body rendered with subtle modelling in the Sittanvasal paintings?
questions.explanation: The body is rendered in yellow with subtle modelling.
questions.subject: Fine Arts
